NVDA.name: NVIDIA Corporation
NVDA.note: Founder-CEO since 1993; world's most valuable company on AI/Rubin-platform boom

AAPL.name: Apple Inc.
AAPL.note: Sitting CEO; Apple announced Apr 20, 2026 that John Ternus becomes CEO Sept 1, 2026, with Cook moving to executive chairman
AAPL.prior_rationale: Ternus transition announced Apr 2026, effective Sep 1 2026 (before horizon)

GOOGL.name: Alphabet Inc.
GOOGL.note: Google CEO since 2015, Alphabet CEO since Dec 2019; 2026 pay package tied to Waymo/Wing moonshots

MSFT.name: Microsoft Corporation
MSFT.note: CEO since 2014 and chairman since 2021; repositioning Microsoft/OpenAI alliance amid intensified AI competition

AMZN.name: Amazon.com, Inc.
AMZN.note: Succeeded founder Jeff Bezos in 2021; defending a $200B 2026 AI capex program

AVGO.name: Broadcom Inc.
AVGO.note: CEO since 2006 (Avago); custom AI chip boom (incl. Anthropic partnership), targets >$100B AI chip revenue in 2027

SPCX.name: Space Exploration Technologies Corp.
SPCX.note: Founder-CEO; largest-ever IPO June 12, 2026 (Nasdaq: SPCX, ~$1.77T valuation); Musk keeps ~82% voting power via Class B shares and stock has slipped back to the $135 IPO price

META.name: Meta Platforms, Inc.
META.note: Founder-CEO with dual-class voting control; betting on Meta Superintelligence Labs and possible cloud business

TSLA.name: Tesla, Inc.
TSLA.note: CEO since 2008; now also CEO of newly public SpaceX, keeping attention-split concerns alive as Tesla pivots to AI/robotics/energy

BRK.B.name: Berkshire Hathaway Inc.
BRK.B.note: Recent succession: Abel became President and CEO Jan 1, 2026, ending Warren Buffett's 60-year run; Buffett remains chairman

LLY.name: Eli Lilly and Company
LLY.note: Chair and CEO since 2017; steered Lilly to ~$1T market cap on GLP-1/obesity franchise, oral orforglipron launching 2026

MU.name: Micron Technology, Inc.
MU.note: CEO since 2017; stock up ~9x in a year on AI memory/HBM boom, crossed $1T market cap, $250B US fab expansion announced July 2026

JPM.name: JPMorgan Chase & Co.
JPM.note: CEO since 2006; succession race narrowed June 2026 to co-presidents Doug Petno and Troy Rohrbaugh after Marianne Lake's exit — Dimon expects ~3 more years
JPM.prior_rationale: succession race narrowed June 2026; Dimon signals ~3 more years

WMT.name: Walmart Inc.
WMT.note: Recent succession: Furner (32-year Walmart veteran, ex-Walmart US CEO) took over Feb 1, 2026 as Doug McMillon retired

AMD.name: Advanced Micro Devices, Inc.
AMD.note: Chair and CEO since 2014; ~40x stock turnaround, keynoted CES 2026 on AI roadmap

V.name: Visa Inc.
V.note: CEO since Feb 2023 (succeeded Al Kelly); elected US-China Business Council board chair June 2026

XOM.name: Exxon Mobil Corporation
XOM.note: Chairman and CEO since Jan 2017; navigating Iran-war oil price spikes and public friction with Trump over Venezuela

JNJ.name: Johnson & Johnson
JNJ.note: CEO since Jan 2022, chairman since 2023; on track for first-ever $100B revenue year in 2026

INTC.name: Intel Corporation
INTC.note: Recent succession: ex-Cadence chief appointed March 18, 2025 after Gelsinger's ouster, driving foundry-led turnaround; Craig Barratt elected independent board chair May 2026

MA.name: Mastercard Incorporated
MA.note: CEO since Jan 2021 (succeeded Ajay Banga); reshuffled leadership June 2026, promoting Ling Hai to CFO

AMAT.name: Applied Materials, Inc.
AMAT.note: Long-tenured CEO (since Sept 2013); no succession announced; calls this 'the greatest time ever' for semis (May 2026)
AMAT.prior_rationale: CEO since 2013, no succession plan announced

CSCO.name: Cisco Systems, Inc.
CSCO.note: Chair & CEO 11+ years; steered shift from hardware to software/subscriptions; active as of June 2026

BAC.name: Bank of America Corporation
BAC.note: Longest-tenured big-bank CEO; Feb 2026 co-president reshuffle (Athanasia, DeMare, Borthwick) set up succession race, but he plans to stay for years
BAC.prior_rationale: Feb 2026 co-president reshuffle set up succession race

ABBV.name: AbbVie Inc.
ABBV.note: Recent succession: took over from Richard Gonzalez July 2024, added chairman July 2025

CAT.name: Caterpillar Inc.
CAT.note: Recent succession: 28-year CAT veteran, CEO May 1 2025 succeeding Jim Umpleby, elected board chairman April 2026

LRCX.name: Lam Research Corporation
LRCX.note: CEO since Jan 2018; Feb 2026 leadership shuffle was COO-level (Varadarajan in, Lord retired) — Archer unchanged

COST.name: Costco Wholesale Corporation
COST.note: Succeeded Craig Jelinek Jan 2024; rose from forklift driver, no college degree; still CEO as of June 2026

ORCL.name: Oracle Corporation
ORCL.note: Co-CEOs since Sept 2025; Safra Catz moved to executive vice chair, Larry Ellison remains chairman/CTO; stock well off its 2025 AI-frenzy highs
ORCL.condition_note: departure condition met if EITHER named co-CEO ceases to hold the office

UNH.name: UnitedHealth Group Incorporated
UNH.note: Boomerang CEO: returned May 2025 after Andrew Witty stepped down (prior CEO 2006-2017); also board chair; testified to Congress Jan 2026

GE.name: GE Aerospace
GE.note: GE CEO since Oct 2018; has led GE Aerospace as standalone since April 2024 spin; employment agreement extended through end-2027

CVX.name: Chevron Corporation
CVX.note: Chairman & CEO since Feb 2018; board waived age-65 retirement rule; succession talks underway but no successor named as of mid-2026

MS.name: Morgan Stanley
MS.note: Succeeded James Gorman Jan 2024, added chairman Jan 2025; delivered record Q1 2026 results

KO.name: The Coca-Cola Company
KO.note: Very recent succession: former COO became CEO March 31 2026, succeeding James Quincey (now executive chairman)

PG.name: The Procter & Gamble Company
PG.note: Recent succession: former COO became CEO Jan 1 2026, succeeding Jon Moeller (now executive chairman); P&G lifer since 1989

HD.name: The Home Depot, Inc.
HD.note: Chair, president & CEO since March 2022 (chairman since Oct 2022); no succession announced

GS.name: The Goldman Sachs Group, Inc.
GS.note: Chairman & CEO since Oct 2018 (chairman 2019); earlier activist/board noise has faded after strong 2025

PLTR.name: Palantir Technologies Inc.
PLTR.note: Founder-CEO (co-founded 2003, CEO since 2004); stock down ~27% in 2026 despite ~85% YoY revenue growth; outspoken vs OpenAI/Anthropic

NFLX.name: Netflix, Inc.
NFLX.note: Co-CEO structure since Jan 2023 (Sarandos co-CEO since 2020); founder Reed Hastings left the board June 2026; share price reflects 2025 10-for-1 split
NFLX.condition_note: departure condition met if EITHER named co-CEO ceases to hold the office

MRK.name: Merck & Co., Inc.
MRK.note: Chairman & CEO (CEO July 2021, chairman Dec 2022); navigating Keytruda patent-cliff narrative

KLAC.name: KLA Corporation
KLAC.note: One of the longest-tenured semicap CEOs (since Jan 2006); no successor announced as of mid-2026

PANW.name: Palo Alto Networks, Inc.
PANW.note: Chairman & CEO since June 2018 (ex-Google/SoftBank); active in July 2026 press (CNBC interview July 9, 2026)

GEV.name: GE Vernova Inc.
GEV.note: CEO since the April 2024 spin-off from GE; led GE's energy businesses before separation

PM.name: Philip Morris International Inc.
PM.note: CEO since May 2021; title redesignated 'Group CEO PMI' effective Jan 1, 2026 in an org restructure

TXN.name: Texas Instruments Incorporated
TXN.note: President & CEO since 2023; added chairman Jan 2026 after Rich Templeton retired end of 2025

DELL.name: Dell Technologies Inc.
DELL.note: Founder-CEO (founded 1984; CEO again since 2007); confirmed Chairman & CEO in FY2026 proxy

WFC.name: Wells Fargo & Company
WFC.note: CEO since Oct 2019, added chairman Oct 2025; led bank out of Fed asset cap (lifted 2025)

RTX.name: RTX Corporation
RTX.note: President & CEO since May 2024 (succeeded Greg Hayes), elected chairman April 2025

AXP.name: American Express Company
AXP.note: Chairman & CEO since Feb 2018; age 67, says no plans to retire, no successor named (May 2026)

SNDK.name: Sandisk Corporation
SNDK.note: Chairman & CEO since Feb 2025 spin-off from Western Digital (was WDC CEO); stock up hugely on AI NAND demand

C.name: Citigroup Inc.
C.note: CEO since March 2021, elected board chair Oct 2025; multi-year turnaround credited, #1 on Fortune MPW 2026

ANET.name: Arista Networks, Inc.
ANET.note: Chairperson & CEO since 2008 (pre-IPO); one of the longest-tenured big-cap tech CEOs, still active July 2026

CRWD.name: CrowdStrike Holdings, Inc.
CRWD.note: Co-founder & CEO since founding in 2011; retained role through and after the July 2024 outage

TMUS.name: T-Mobile US, Inc.
TMUS.note: Recent succession: became CEO Nov 1, 2025, succeeding Mike Sievert (now vice chairman); ex-Deutsche Telekom Germany CEO

TMO.name: Thermo Fisher Scientific Inc.
TMO.note: Chairman & CEO; CEO since 2009, ceded the President title in early 2026 (remains Chairman & CEO per 2026 proxy)
TMO.prior_rationale: ceded President title early 2026 — classic succession precursor

IBM.name: International Business Machines Corporation
IBM.note: CEO since April 2020, chairman since 2021; opened IBM Think 2026, pushing $10B+ quantum investment

AMGN.name: Amgen Inc.
AMGN.note: Chairman & CEO; CEO since 2012, chairman since 2013; signed 2025 shareholder letter March 2026, big MariTide capex

APH.name: Amphenol Corporation
APH.note: CEO since 2009; added chairman at the May 2026 annual meeting as long-time chairman Martin Loeffler retired

ADI.name: Analog Devices, Inc.
ADI.note: CEO since May 2013, board chair since 2022; touting record FY2026 results and AI infrastructure demand

MCD.name: McDonald's Corporation
MCD.note: CEO since Nov 2019 (post-Easterbrook), chairman since 2024; driving ~50,000-restaurant goal by 2027

QCOM.name: QUALCOMM Incorporated
QCOM.note: President & CEO since June 2021; diversifying beyond smartphones into auto/edge-AI, keynoted COMPUTEX 2026

NEE.name: NextEra Energy, Inc.
NEE.note: Chairman/CEO since March 2022; announced $67B merger with Dominion Energy in May 2026 and will lead the combined company

MRVL.name: Marvell Technology, Inc.
MRVL.note: Chairman & CEO since July 2016; delivered COMPUTEX 2026 keynote on AI connectivity

PEP.name: PepsiCo, Inc.
PEP.note: Chairman/CEO since 2018; no announced successor as of July 2026 despite activist pressure (Elliott stake, 2025); joined IBM board March 2026

VZ.name: Verizon Communications Inc.
VZ.note: Recent succession: ex-PayPal CEO and Verizon board member replaced Hans Vestberg in October 2025; Vestberg is special advisor through October 2026

SCHW.name: The Charles Schwab Corporation
SCHW.note: Recent succession: promoted from president, succeeded Walt Bettinger as CEO effective January 1, 2025

BLK.name: BlackRock, Inc.
BLK.note: Co-founder-CEO since 1988; says he is not leaving soon while succession bench has thinned (Wiedman and Ramji departed)

WDC.name: Western Digital Corporation
WDC.note: CEO since February 2025 after the SanDisk flash spinoff; stock up sharply on AI-driven HDD demand, capacity 'pretty much sold out' for 2026

BA.name: The Boeing Company
BA.note: Turnaround hire (ex-Rockwell Collins), CEO since August 2024; 737 MAX output cap lifted to 42/month with 52/month targeted by end of 2026

UNP.name: Union Pacific Corporation
UNP.note: CEO since August 2023; leading the pending ~$72B Norfolk Southern merger now under STB review

DIS.name: The Walt Disney Company
DIS.note: Brand-new succession: former Disney Experiences (parks) chief became CEO March 18, 2026, ending Bob Iger's tenure; Iger stays as senior adviser through 2026

TJX.name: The TJX Companies, Inc.
TJX.note: CEO since January 2016; long-tenured off-price veteran, annual sales passed $60B in FY2026

IBKR.name: Interactive Brokers Group, Inc.
IBKR.note: 30+ year insider, CEO since Q3 2019; founder Thomas Peterffy remains chairman and controlling holder — the ~$43B market cap is the public Class A float only, whole-company value is far larger

WELL.name: Welltower Inc.
WELL.note: CEO since October 2020; awarded an ~$821M long-term stock package, ranking second among S&P 500 CEOs in 2025 pay behind only Musk

GILD.name: Gilead Sciences, Inc.
GILD.note: Chairman & CEO since March 2019, ex-Roche Pharmaceuticals chief; no transition announced

DE.name: Deere & Company
DE.note: Chairman & CEO since November 2019; new CFO appointed May 2026 but no CEO transition announced

ABT.name: Abbott Laboratories
ABT.note: CEO since March 2020, chairman since December 2021; bought ~$2M of ABT stock personally in 2026 on share weakness

BX.name: Blackstone Inc.
BX.note: Co-founder-CEO since founding in 1985; president/COO Jon Gray is the designated heir apparent; firm crossed $1.3T AUM in Q1 2026

APP.name: AppLovin Corporation
APP.note: Founder-CEO since the company's 2011-2012 founding; handed off the board chair role in April 2026 but remains CEO

SCCO.name: Southern Copper Corporation
SCCO.note: Appointed CEO April 23, 2026 after longtime CEO Oscar Gonzalez Rocha died April 7, 2026; company is Grupo Mexico-controlled (German Larrea chairman)

GLW.name: Corning Incorporated
GLW.note: Chairman/CEO since April 2005, one of the longest-tenured S&P 500 CEOs; stock roughly tripled on AI optical demand incl. $6B Meta partnership

T.name: AT&T Inc.
T.note: CEO since July 2020, added chairman Feb 2025; driving fiber expansion incl. Lumen mass-market fiber deal closed Feb 2026

UBER.name: Uber Technologies, Inc.
UBER.note: Post-Kalanick turnaround CEO since 2017; stepped off Grab board July 2026, pushing autonomous-vehicle rollout

DHR.name: Danaher Corporation
DHR.note: Internal promotion (ex-Life Sciences EVP), President & CEO since Sept 2020; Danaher Business System steward

BKNG.name: Booking Holdings Inc.
BKNG.note: Company veteran (joined 2000), CEO since Jan 2017; share price reflects 25-for-1 stock split effected April 2026

PFE.name: Pfizer Inc.
PFE.note: Chairman & CEO since 2019; navigating post-Covid patent cliff with obesity/GLP-1 pipeline push

ISRG.name: Intuitive Surgical, Inc.
ISRG.note: Recent succession: 27-year insider succeeded Gary Guthart as CEO July 2025; Guthart now executive chair

PLD.name: Prologis, Inc.
PLD.note: Recent succession: became CEO Jan 1, 2026 after multi-year handover from co-founder Hamid Moghadam, now executive chairman

CRM.name: Salesforce, Inc.
CRM.note: Co-founder, Chair & CEO (prior co-CEO stints with Block and Taylor ended); defending AI strategy and buybacks amid 2026 stock slump
CRM.prior_rationale: AI strategy + buyback defense amid 2026 stock slump

COP.name: ConocoPhillips
COP.note: Chairman & CEO since May 2012; faced 2026 shareholder proposal seeking an independent board chair

CVS.name: CVS Health Corporation
CVS.note: Recent succession: PBM veteran replaced Karen Lynch Oct 2024 amid investor pressure; added board chair Jan 1, 2026

SPGI.name: S&P Global Inc.
SPGI.note: Recent succession: ex-Ratings president succeeded Doug Peterson as President & CEO in Nov 2024

COF.name: Capital One Financial Corporation
COF.note: Co-founder-CEO since 1994 IPO (chairman since 1995); integrated Discover onto Capital One's platform by Jan 2026

SYK.name: Stryker Corporation
SYK.note: Chair & CEO since Oct 2012 (chair 2014); 60+ acquisitions in tenure; joined GE HealthCare board Mar 2026

VRTX.name: Vertex Pharmaceuticals Incorporated
VRTX.note: President & CEO since April 2020; first female CEO of a large-cap US biotech; TIME 2026 Women of the Year

FTNT.name: Fortinet, Inc.
FTNT.note: Founder-CEO and chairman since co-founding Fortinet in Oct 2000 (with brother Michael Xie)

PH.name: Parker-Hannifin Corporation
PH.note: First female CEO of Parker (Jan 2023), added board chair 2024; ex-COO promoted internally; record FY2026 results

BMY.name: Bristol-Myers Squibb Company
BMY.note: CEO since Nov 2023, board chair since Apr 2024; managing patent-cliff pivot to Growth Portfolio ($26.4B 2025 sales)

SBUX.name: Starbucks Corporation
SBUX.note: Recent succession: poached from Chipotle as chairman & CEO Sept 2024; 'Back to Starbucks' turnaround showing gains in 2026

PGR.name: The Progressive Corporation
PGR.note: President & CEO since July 2016, first woman to lead Progressive; started as claims rep in 1988; CFO transition (Sauerland to Quigg) July 2026

LMT.name: Lockheed Martin Corporation
LMT.note: Ex-American Tower CEO; President & CEO since June 2020, chairman since Mar 2021; scaling missile output on defense-spending surge
